data.title: 'Hold Your Horses,' Says Morgan Stanley as CoreWeave Stock (CRWV) Faces a Downgrade
data.description:
CoreWeave (NASDAQ:CRWV) shares head into their February 26 earnings report with a lot already priced in. The AI cloud infrastructure provider has been one of the market's biggest beneficiaries of the generative AI buildout, riding demand from large language model developers and hyperscalers scrambling for access to high-performance GPUs. So far this year, the stock has climbed 25%, outperforming large-cap software peers, which have struggled through a choppier stretch.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Investors have grown accustomed to a steady drumbeat of upbeat updates from the company, particularly through the middle of last year, when deal announcements, new power contracts, capital raises, and M&A activity kept sentiment elevated between earnings reports. This time around, however, the fourth quarter has been comparatively quiet, raising the stakes for the upcoming print.

That is where Morgan Stanley analyst Keith Weiss steps in with a word of caution. Downgrading the stock today from Overweight (i.e., Buy) to Equal-weight (i.e., Neutral), the analyst urges investors to take a breath. After what he calls an "underwhelming Q3" that raised doubts about CoreWeave's execution in a supply-constrained environment, the stock's rally leaves expectations running high into Q4.

Weiss argues this creates "a reasonably difficult setup," one in which management needs to thread the needle on several fronts. Chief among them is power. Active power stood at 590 megawatts at the end of Q3, and the company has guided to exiting fiscal 2025 with more than 850 MW online. According to Weiss, surpassing that 850 MW mark is critical because it "gives credence to the durability of the growth story." Investors will be watching closely for updates on incremental power delivery, progress on resolving prior data center delays, and clarity around the recently discussed goal of contracting an additional 5 gigawatts of power by 2030.

Backlog and bookings are another focal point. In Q3, backlog-based bookings of $6.165 billion fell short of expectations despite a widely discussed $6 billion-plus deal with Nvidia. With fewer large announcements during Q4, Weiss says it becomes harder to triangulate expectations, increasing the importance of evidence that bookings growth extends beyond mega-deals and reflects a broader customer base.

Looking further out, Weiss sees a constructive setup for 2026, with 2.9 GW of contracted capacity expected to come online over the next 12 to 24 months and capital expenditures projected to more than double year over year. Still, the analyst cautions that revenue guidance likely needs to come in above consensus to rebuild confidence, particularly given the company has missed capex targets in consecutive quarters.

Investors reacted swiftly to the more cautious tone, sending CoreWeave shares down 8% as of this writing, with the stock trading around $89. With Weiss maintaining a $99 price target, the upside from here stands at ~12%. (To watch Weiss' track record, click here)

Wall Street, on balance, still sees more upside than downside. CoreWeave holds a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on 17 recent analyst reviews, with 9 analysts recommending the stock and 8 taking a more neutral stance. The average 12-month price target of $118.57 implies about 33% upside from current levels. (See CRWV stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: Blue Owl Capital Stock Sinks on Reports of a Failed Data Center Funding Deal with CoreWeave (CRWV)
data.description:
Blue Owl Capital (OWL) stock took a beating on Friday alongside reports that the asset manager failed to secure funding for a data center project. According to this report, Blue Owl Capital was unable to obtain $4 billion in funding for a data center project in Pennsylvania that would be built with the help of cloud company CoreWeave (CRWV).

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Blue Owl Capital has increased its focus on data centers recently, which have become a much-needed resource to power artificial intelligence (AI). The company has already signed a $27 billion deal with Meta Platforms (META) to fund one of the company's biggest data centers.

Blue Owl Capital was also recently in the news after it announced plans to return capital from a debt fund to investors. Earlier this week, the company said it would sell $1.4 billion worth of assets from three funds to return the proceeds to investors. This news shook confidence in the company and other alternative investment firms, kicking off negative momentum for OWL stock.

Blue Owl Capital Stock Movement Today

Blue Owl Capital stock was down 2.51% on Friday, extending a 25.6% drop year-to-date. The company's shares have also fallen 48.69% over the past 12 months.

OWL stock trading activity today saw some 14 million shares change hands. The company will likely surpass its three-month daily average trading volume of 20.37 million units before the end of trading today.
data.author: William White

data.title: ETHQ, ETC, TKN: Canada Is Home to Innovative Crypto ETFs
data.description:
There's more to cryptocurrency exchange-traded funds (ETFs) than Bitcoin (BTC) and Ethereum (ETH).

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

While most investors focus on ETFs that track the spot prices of BTC and ETH, the two largest cryptocurrencies by market capitalization, there are many more options available to investors willing to look further afield.

Canada is home to a wide variety of crypto ETFs that go further than Bitcoin and Ethereum. In fact, Canadian regulators approved spot Bitcoin and Ethereum ETFs before regulators in the U.S. The northern country continues to be proactive on crypto trading, and investors can find diversity within the Canadian market.

Canadian Crypto ETFs

Innovative crypto ETFs to consider include the 3iQ Ether Staking ETF (ETHQ). This Canadian ETF invests in Ethereum and has staked a portion of the Ether held in the ETF to provide investors with yield. There's also the Evolve Cryptocurrencies ETF (ETC) that provides exposure to Bitcoin, Ethereum, Solana (SOL), and XRP (XRP).

For investors also interested in artificial intelligence (AI), there's the Ninepoint Crypto and AI Leaders ETF (TKN) that focuses on emerging technologies and gives investors exposure to Web3, blockchain, and digital asset products. Below is a chart comparing these three crypto ETFs.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Trump's Tariffs Struck Down by Supreme Court in 6-3 Ruling
data.description:
The Supreme Court announced Friday that it had struck down President Trump's tariffs in a 6‑3 ruling. SCOTUS said Trump exceeded his executive power when invoking the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA) to enforce sweeping "reciprocal" tariffs. The act allows the president to impose economic sanctions, including tariffs, on foreign nations in response to declared national emergencies.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"IEEPA does not authorize the President to impose tariffs," read the ruling.

Supreme Court Ruling Puts $175 Billion of Tariff Refunds on the Line

In February 2025, Trump imposed tariffs on Canada, China and Mexico for failing to prevent the flow of fentanyl in the U.S. In April, he imposed 10% tariffs on nearly all countries, with higher rates targeting those deemed to engage in unfair trade practices with the U.S.

Since then, the U.S. has collected $175 billion of tariff revenue, according to an estimate from Penn-Wharton Budget Model economists. In other words, $175 billion of tariff refunds could be up for grabs.

However, the ruling noted that the "Court says nothing today about whether, and if so how, the Government should go about returning the billions of dollars that it has collected from importers." This complicates any potential refund process and could require another ruling.

data.title: S&P 500 Stumbles as GDP Growth Loses Steam
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SPX) opened Friday in negative territory following a sharp slowdown in fourth-quarter gross domestic product (GDP).

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Data from the Bureau of Economic Analysis showed that the U.S. economy grew at an annualized rate of 1.4% during the last three months of 2025, missing the estimate of 3% and down significantly from 4.4% in the third quarter. For the entire year, GDP rose by 2.2%, down from 2.8% in 2024 and marking the weakest rate since 2020.

Government Shutdown And Falling Exports Weigh On GDP

During the fourth quarter, consumer spending and investment contributed to GDP growth, while the government shutdown and falling exports dragged it down.

"The Democrat Shutdown cost the U.S.A. at least two points in GDP. That's why they are doing it, in mini form, again. No Shutdowns!" Trump said in a Truth Social post.

Government spending fell at an annualized rate of 5.1% in the fourth quarter, resulting in a 0.9% hit to GDP. At the same time, real final sales to private domestic purchasers, which is the sum of consumer spending and gross private fixed investment, remained strong at 2.4% compared to 2.9% in the third quarter.

data.title: Analysts Shower Deere Stock (DE) with Price Target Increases Post Earnings
data.description:
Deere (DE) stock was a hot topic among analysts after the company smashed Wall Street's estimates in its most recent earnings report. That sent its shares soaring more than 11% yesterday, and triggered several analyst updates.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Here's what Wall Street's top analysts had to say about Deere after its earnings report:

  • Five-star J.P. Morgan analyst Tami Zakaria reiterated a Hold rating but increased her price target to $525 from $460, representing a potential 20.69% downside.
  • Five-star RBC Capital analyst Sabahat Khan maintained a Buy rating and boosted his price target to $736 from $541, suggesting a possible 11.18% upside.
  • Five-star Bank of America analyst Michael Feniger kept a Hold rating but raised his price target to $672 from $502, implying a 1.51% upside.
  • Five-star Truist Financial analyst Jamie Cook stuck with a Buy rating and increased her price target to a Street-high $793 from $612, representing a potential 19.79% upside.
  • Five-star Robert W. Baird analyst Mircea Dobre reiterated a Hold rating and boosted his price target to $675 from $467, suggesting a 1.96% upside.
  • Five-star UBS analyst Steven Fisher maintained a Buy rating and raised his price target to $775 from $535, implying a 17.07% upside.
  • Four-star Oppenheimer analyst Kristen Owen kept a Buy rating and increased her price target to $715 from $531, representing a possible 8.01% upside.

Deere Stock Movement Today

Deere stock was down 1.42% on Friday, but was still up 40.17% year-to-date. The shares have also rallied 35.11% over the past 12 months.
data.author: William White

data.title: Citigroup (C) Reveals Its 'AI Survival Plan:' Buy Bonds and Small-Cap Stocks
data.description:
U.S. bank Citigroup (C) is providing investors with an artificial intelligence (AI) survival plan.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The Wall Street lender says investors can hedge against both a bursting of the AI bubble and an AI-driven labor market disruption by rotating capital into bonds and small-cap stocks. Dirk Willer, Global Head of Macro Strategy at Citi, says those are the smartest moves for investors to make as market volatility worsens.

In a note to clients, Willer says that "better diversification is essential" now as investors move away from the AI trade. Citigroup's recommended global portfolio is now half in small-cap stocks. Willer adds that U.S. Treasurys should also work as a safe haven amid a gathering market storm.

Why Small-Cap Stocks Work

Citigroup says that small-cap stocks are likely to work moving forward as their valuations tend to be lower than those of richly-valued technology stocks. Also, small-cap stocks are far removed from the AI trade and its negative impacts.

Small-cap stocks are also likely to get a big bounce from future interest rate cuts. Citigroup is currently pricing in three rate cuts from the U.S. Federal Reserve this year. As for bonds, Citi has shifted to overweight on long-term inflation-protected bonds, which tend to work when the Fed is lowering rates.

Is C Stock a Buy?

Citigroup's stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 18 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 15 Buy and three Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average C price target of $131.94 implies 14.18% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Here's Why Mangoceuticals Stock (MGRX) Rocketed Today
data.description:
Mangoceuticals (MGRX) stock underwent a massive rally on Friday after the men's wellness company announced successful results from its new Testosterone Replacement Therapy (TRT) treatment program. This program offers all-inclusive injectable TRT, doctor visits, medical consultations, lab work and analysis for $99 per month.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Mangoceuticals noted that its month-over-month sales for its injectable TRT treatment program have increased 336% since it launched in mid-December. Additionally, the company said that it has reduced customer acquisition costs by 54%. Mangoceuticals said it expects the injectable TRT treatment program to be its primary growth driver moving forward.

Jacob Cohen, Founder and CEO of Mangoceuticals, said, "By combining effective TRT solutions with streamlined telehealth delivery and targeted cost efficiencies, we are seeking to become better positioned to support more men seeking reliable, discreet care. At the same time, we are committed to building a more comprehensive platform that delivers a wider range of wellness solutions, which we expect to strengthen our operational and financial foundation and position us for sustained growth."

Mangoceuticals Stock Movement Today

Mangoceuticals stock was up 68.86% in pre-market trading on Friday, following a 1.08% drop yesterday. The shares have fallen 51.88% year-to-date and 91.78% over the past 12 months.

With today's news came heavy trading of MGRX stock. This saw more than 122 million shares change hands as of this writing. For comparison, the stock's three-month daily average trading volume is about 208,000 units.
data.author: William White
